title: feat(bhaga): read-only status doctor CLI + anti-drift enforcement
requirement:
Build a reusable, token-cheap way to answer recurring BHAGA/Jarvis operational questions (e.g. "did yesterday's incremental run land in Sheets, BQ, and Grafana?") so a fresh chat on any machine never has to re-derive coordinates or hand-write verification queries. Two deliverables: (A) a read-only ops "doctor" CLI, and (B) wire it into the rules as the standard first check.

CONTEXT (already derived — don't re-investigate): The BHAGA nightly pipeline (agents/bhaga/scripts/daily_refresh.py) advances data through three layers: Google Sheets (source of truth) -> BigQuery mirror -> Grafana Cloud (reads BQ vw_* views). Coordinates: GCP project jarvis-bhaga-prod, BQ dataset `jarvis-bhaga-prod.bhaga`. Model sheet ID + raw sheet IDs come from agents/bhaga/knowledge-base/store-profiles/palmetto.json (gitignored PII — resolve at runtime, never hardcode). Auth pattern that works locally: BHAGA_SECRETS_BACKEND=gcp + BHAGA_IMPERSONATE_SA=bhaga-orchestrator@jarvis-bhaga-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com, then core.config_loader.refresh_access_token('palmetto') for Sheets and a google.cloud.bigquery.Client(project='jarvis-bhaga-prod') for BQ. Read sheet tabs via skills/tip_ledger_writer/writer._read_tab; the prod-read guard only blocks under BHAGA_SHEET_MODE=staging. RUNBOOK.md section 14 lists the model_* tables and vw_* views.

DELIVERABLE A — agents/bhaga/scripts/status.py (module: python3 -m agents.bhaga.scripts.status --store palmetto [--date YYYY-MM-DD]). Default --date = yesterday in America/Chicago. For the date it prints a compact freshness table across all three layers and a one-line verdict, exiting nonzero if any layer is missing the date (so it is alert/CI usable):
  - Sheets: model config tab data_window_end; presence + row counts of the date in `daily` and `tip_alloc_daily`.
  - BigQuery: max date + row count for the date in model_daily, model_labor_daily, model_tip_alloc_daily, model_period_summary, and raw square_transactions/adp_shifts/adp_punches/square_daily_rollup (note model_labor_weekly is keyed by iso_week — compare the ISO week, not the literal date).
  - Grafana BI contract: confirm the date is present in the exact views Grafana renders (vw_daily_sales, vw_model_labor_daily, vw_model_period_summary, vw_labor_daily, vw_tips_by_hour, vw_sales_labor_daily). Print the dashboard URL (steadyangelfish2985.grafana.net/d/bhaga-analytics-v1) in the output. Do NOT browser-render Grafana; data-layer confirmation of its views is the contract.
  Reuse core.datastore for BQ reads where it fits; resolve all IDs from the store profile. Read-only only — never write to any prod data source (honor the bhaga-principles sandbox/prod-write guards).

DELIVERABLE B — discovery wiring so a cold agent finds it without spelunking: add a one-liner to .cursor/rules/bhaga-principles.md (always-on card) and the script catalog in agents/bhaga/scripts/README.md: "To check whether a run landed in Sheets/BQ/Grafana, run `status` first — don't hand-investigate." Wire it as the standard first operational check.

ALSO: add tests (agents/bhaga/scripts/test_status.py) mocking the readers + BQ client (follow test_update_model_sheet.py / verify_bq_parity.py patterns; no live network in tests). Keep docs in lock-step: RUNBOOK.md section 14 + scripts/README.md + a dated PROGRESS.md entry, and register this as a new row in Playground/REQUIREMENTS.md via scripts/requirements_tracker.py (mark In Progress with the PR). Follow CONTRIBUTING.md: branch -> PR -> Claude review -> CI -> merge -> deploy; prove with the sandbox e2e, never by touching prod sheets.

build.source: cursor dashboard usage API (conversation-scoped via ai_code_hashes)
